question: How can you know about the current status with different LED indicator behavior on the base station of a landline?
content: For the XL495 base station, read the LED as a message/status indicator: steady on means there is no new message and the memory is not full, the answering machine is switched on, or paging mode is being entered. Flashing means there is a new message and memory is not full, an incoming call, recording of an incoming message or announcement, message playback, or remote access/listening to messages from the handset. Fast flashing means there is no new message and the memory is full, or registration mode is being entered.<PIC>
ret: "For the XL495 base station, read the LED as a message/status indicator: steady on means there is no new message and the memory is not full, the answering machine is switched on, or paging mode is being entered. Flashing means there is a new message and memory is not full, an incoming call, recording of an incoming message or announcement, message playback, or remote access/listening to messages from the handset. Fast flashing means there is no new message and the memory is full, or registration mode is being entered.<PIC>",["Manual22_46"]
